sourceMovie.title: The Wolfman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Wolfman for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Joe Johnston keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Bram Stoker's Dracula
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wolfman clicked for you, Bram Stoker's Dracula (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wolfman?
faq.answer: Try Bram Stoker's Dracula, Nosferatu, Frankenstein—each shares DNA with The Wolfman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wolfman?
faq.answer: The Wolfman (2010) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wolfman worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
